page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is a compute-focused virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 180 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses, and operates on the x86_64 architecture.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across various computational workloads. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate matrix operations and cryptography. In testing, the instance achieved a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2175.0 and a strong multi-core score of 18084.0. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 14.47 GB/sec, placing it in the top tier, while multi-threaded decompression registered at 288.36 GB/sec.

Memory performance is characterized by a strong cached read bandwidth of 3389.0 GB/sec for small blocks, though uncached large-block read and write speeds drop to average levels of 144.84 GB/sec and 61.98 GB/sec respectively. For specialized tasks, the platform demonstrates strong efficiency in LLM inference, processing 198.07 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a medium 7B model, and 367.28 tokens/sec for text generation on a small 135M model.

Given its high compute density and advanced instruction support, the c4-standard-48 is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, image processing, and database operations. Its qualitative cost efficiency is driven by the high resource density of the Intel Xeon platform, making it a viable option for enterprise applications requiring dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of local storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance configured with 48 vCPUs and 180 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Lacking local storage, this instance relies on dedicated CPU allocation with 24 physical cores. It features advanced hardware capabilities including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which drive strong performance in cryptographic, mathematical, and vector-based operations. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core Geekbench performance and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities. The instance is highly efficient for workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is a compute-focused instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with AMX and AVX-512 support for high-performance workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 features 48 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong performance for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring advanced matrix extensions and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 48 vCPUs and 180 GB RAM
Features Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 for accelerated vector math
Delivers top-tier single-core Geekbench scores and strong multi-threaded decompression
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
No local storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses included
